assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 62-75 detail all 5 criteria as PASS: Criterion 1 (single license filter), Criterion 2 (multi-license filter), Criterion 3 (invalid license returns 400), Criterion 4 (filter integrates with pagination), Criterion 5 (response shape unchanged). Each criterion also has a dedicated file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) with verdict PASS.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match the task specification exactly (2 modified, 1 created); no extra files'. Lines 27-29 show the file-by-file comparison table: list.rs (Modify -> Modified, MATCH), service/mod.rs (Modify -> Modified, MATCH), tests/api/package.rs (Create -> Created, MATCH). Line 31: 'No files outside the task specification were touched.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are report/analysis files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). The report describes reading and analyzing diff content but contains no evidence of file modifications, git commits, or code changes. The outputs consist entirely of verification findings and verdicts.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' but does not mention merging, approving, or taking any merge action. The report presents structured findings (scope containment, security, correctness, style) for human consumption. No merge commands, API calls, or approval actions are referenced anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, secrets, tokens, credentials, or private keys found in the diff'. Lines 47-53 detail the line-by-line scan results: Passwords/hardcoded credentials: None found, API keys/tokens: None found, Secret strings/private keys: None found, Connection strings with embedded credentials: None found, Environment variable references to secrets: None found.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/package.rs` is a new file containing only new test cases'. Lines 88-89: 'Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE - tests/api/package.rs is a newly created file. No existing tests were modified or removed. All 4 tests are additions covering the new license filter feature.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all listed verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS, line 7), Diff Size (PASS, line 8), Commit Traceability (WARN, line 9) under 'Intent Alignment Findings' (line 21); Sensitive Patterns (PASS, line 10) under 'Security Findings' (line 45); CI Status (PASS, line 11), Acceptance Criteria (PASS, line 12), Verification Commands (N/A, line 15) under 'Correctness Findings' (line 57); Test Quality (PASS, line 13), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE, line 14) under 'Style/Conventions Findings' (line 79). All four domain sections are present with their respective verdicts.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 23-41): file-by-file comparison table with Task Specification vs PR Diff columns, per-file line counts (~15, ~10, 80 lines), commit traceability details. Security (lines 47-54): line-by-line scan listing 5 specific pattern categories (passwords, API keys, secret strings, connection strings, env vars) each marked 'None found', plus description of what the diff contains. Correctness (lines 59-75): per-criterion evidence citing specific code constructs (PackageListParams, validate_license_param, is_in filter, test method names and assertions). Style/Conventions (lines 81-89): test quality assessment covering Given/When/Then pattern, naming conventions, project patterns, and classification rationale.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. These two rows appear at the top of the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), and they do not appear under any of the four domain findings sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions). Their placement and independent rationale ('No review comments on the PR', 'No sub-tasks created') confirm they are orchestrator-level determinations separate from domain analysis.
